name: MDR Response
description: Sophos MDR detected a ransomware attack at 2am, isolated affected hosts within 8 minutes, and delivered a full incident report by morning. The customer slept through the entire thing.

items.description: CryptoGuard blocked ransomware encryption on DESKTOP-FIN042 at 2:39 AM. Sophos MDR team was notified immediately. 127 files protected from encryption.
items.info: MDR team responded within 8 minutes. Device isolated at 2:47 AM.

items.description: Command and control beacon detected on DESKTOP-FIN042. Outbound connection to 185.220.101.47:443 established via rundll32.exe. MDR team tracking attacker activity.
items.info: C2 channel identified — MDR monitoring lateral movement attempts

items.description: Lateral movement attempt detected: DESKTOP-FIN042 → SRV-FS01 via SMB using stolen credentials (svc-backup). MDR team blocked at 2:44 AM before file server was compromised.
items.info: Lateral movement blocked — service account credentials compromised

items.description: Initial access: Malicious Excel macro detected in 'Q1_Budget_Review.xlsm' delivered via email. Macro downloaded second-stage payload from compromised WordPress site.
items.info: Delivery vector identified — spearphishing email to sarah.chen@{{customerDomain}}

items.description: MDR remediation complete: DESKTOP-FIN042 cleaned, malicious files removed, persistence mechanisms cleared. Device cleared for reconnection pending customer approval.
items.info: Full remediation performed by Sophos MDR Team

items.name: MDR-{{customerName}}: Ransomware Incident — Contained
items.overview:
INCIDENT SUMMARY

At 01:42 AM, a spearphishing email delivered a malicious Excel macro to sarah.chen (Finance). The macro downloaded a Cobalt Strike beacon establishing C2 with 185.220.101.47. At 02:31 AM, the attacker attempted lateral movement to SRV-FS01 (file server) using stolen svc-backup credentials. At 02:39 AM, ransomware deployment began — CryptoGuard blocked encryption.

MDR RESPONSE
• 02:39 AM — Alert triggered, MDR analyst assigned
• 02:44 AM — Lateral movement blocked, SRV-FS01 protected
• 02:47 AM — DESKTOP-FIN042 isolated from network
• 03:15 AM — C2 IP blocked at firewall (185.220.101.47)
• 03:30 AM — Compromised credentials (svc-backup) flagged for reset
• 04:45 AM — Full remediation complete: malware removed, persistence cleared
• 06:00 AM — Incident report delivered to customer

IMPACT: 1 endpoint compromised (DESKTOP-FIN042). Lateral movement blocked before file server was reached. Zero data loss. Zero files encrypted (CryptoGuard). Estimated attacker dwell time: 57 minutes before containment.

STATUS: Contained. Awaiting customer approval to bring DESKTOP-FIN042 back online.

items.name: (SRV-FS01) | Lateral Movement via SMB — {{customerName}}
items.overview: MDR detected lateral movement from DESKTOP-FIN042 to SRV-FS01 using compromised svc-backup credentials. SMB admin share access was blocked. Credentials flagged for immediate reset. No data accessed on file server.

items.name: (DESKTOP-HR003) | Phishing — Credential Harvesting Blocked
items.overview: Targeted phishing email impersonating Microsoft 365 login sent to HR department. User clicked link but Sophos Web Protection blocked the credential harvesting page. MDR confirmed no credentials were compromised. Sender domain added to block list.

items.name: (SRV-DC01) | Suspicious Scheduled Task — Persistence Attempt
items.overview: Base64-encoded PowerShell scheduled task created on domain controller SRV-DC01. MDR identified this as a persistence mechanism from a previous compromise attempt. Task was removed, system scanned clean. Root cause traced to exposed RDP port — remediation guidance provided.

items.name: (LAPTOP-EXEC01) | Suspicious Data Staging — Exfiltration Prevented
items.overview: MDR detected bulk archiving of financial documents on executive laptop LAPTOP-EXEC01, with attempted upload to personal cloud storage. Upload was blocked by DLP policy. Investigation confirmed the activity was from a compromised account — password reset enforced, MFA enrollment required.

items.ruleDescription: Office application executed macro that downloaded remote payload
Cobalt Strike beacon loaded via rundll32 — C2 connection established to 185.220.101.47
Shadow copy deletion via encoded PowerShell — ransomware preparation

items.action: Case auto-created from high-severity detection: Cobalt Strike C2 beacon on DESKTOP-FIN042
Case escalated to MDR — priority assignment to senior threat analyst

Initial triage complete. Confirmed Cobalt Strike beacon (PID 7842) communicating with C2 at 185.220.101.47:443. Entry vector: spearphishing email with malicious Excel macro delivered to sarah.chen@{{customerDomain}}
Threat hunting initiated across all endpoints. Scanning for lateral movement indicators and additional beacons.
Lateral movement detected: DESKTOP-FIN042 attempted SMB connection to SRV-FS01 using compromised svc-backup credentials. Connection BLOCKED by Sophos.
DESKTOP-FIN042 isolated from network via Sophos API. Device can no longer communicate with internal resources or external C2.
CryptoGuard blocked ransomware encryption attempt on DESKTOP-FIN042. Shadow copy deletion also blocked. Zero files encrypted.
Firewall rule deployed: all outbound traffic to 185.220.101.47 blocked across all network zones.
Compromised credential identified: svc-backup (service account). Flagged for immediate password reset. Kerberos tickets invalidated.
Full malware analysis complete. Artifacts: Q1_Budget_Review.xlsm (macro dropper), update_helper.dll (Cobalt Strike loader), svchost_alt.exe (persistence). All files quarantined.
Persistence mechanisms removed: Scheduled task 'WindowsHealthCheck' deleted, registry run key cleaned, dropped files removed from C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\.
Endpoint scan complete on all 3 affected hosts. No additional indicators of compromise found. Threat contained to DESKTOP-FIN042.

Incident report generated and delivered to customer. Recommendations: (1) Reset svc-backup credentials, (2) Enable MFA on all service accounts, (3) Review email gateway rules for macro-enabled attachments, (4) Bring DESKTOP-FIN042 back online after verification.
Case status updated: CONTAINMENT → awaiting customer approval to restore DESKTOP-FIN042 to network.

items.title: Executive Summary
items.content:
At 01:42 AM, a targeted spearphishing email was delivered to sarah.chen in Finance containing a malicious Excel macro (Q1_Budget_Review.xlsm). The macro downloaded a Cobalt Strike beacon that established C2 communication with 185.220.101.47. The attacker extracted service account credentials (svc-backup) and attempted lateral movement to the file server SRV-FS01. Sophos blocked the lateral movement, CryptoGuard prevented ransomware encryption, and MDR isolated the compromised device within 28 minutes of initial detection. Zero data was lost or encrypted.

Dwell time: 57 minutes (detection to full containment)
Impact: 1 endpoint compromised, 0 files encrypted, 0 data exfiltrated
Status: Contained, awaiting customer approval to restore device

items.title: Attack Timeline
items.content:
01:42 AM — Spearphishing email delivered to sarah.chen@{{customerDomain}}
01:43 AM — User opens Q1_Budget_Review.xlsm, macro executes
01:44 AM — PowerShell downloads Cobalt Strike stager from 185.220.101.47
01:45 AM — Cobalt Strike beacon established (PID 7842, rundll32.exe)
02:15 AM — Mimikatz module extracts svc-backup credentials from LSASS
02:31 AM — Lateral movement attempt: SMB to SRV-FS01 using svc-backup — BLOCKED
02:39 AM — Ransomware deployment begins — CryptoGuard BLOCKS encryption
02:39 AM — Shadow copy deletion attempted — BLOCKED
02:39 AM — MDR alert triggered, analyst assigned
02:44 AM — MDR confirms Cobalt Strike, begins containment
02:47 AM — DESKTOP-FIN042 isolated from network
03:15 AM — C2 IP 185.220.101.47 blocked at firewall
03:30 AM — svc-backup credentials flagged for reset
04:00 AM — Malware analysis complete, all artifacts quarantined
04:30 AM — Persistence mechanisms removed
05:00 AM — Full endpoint scan clean
06:00 AM — Incident report delivered to customer

items.title: Recommendations
items.content:
IMMEDIATE ACTIONS (within 24 hours):
1. Reset svc-backup service account password and rotate all associated Kerberos tickets
2. Enable MFA on all service accounts and admin accounts
3. Review and restrict SMB access between workstation and server segments
4. Block macro-enabled email attachments at the email gateway

SHORT-TERM (within 1 week):
5. Conduct phishing awareness training focused on Finance department
6. Implement application whitelisting on critical servers (SRV-FS01)
7. Deploy network segmentation between workstation and server VLANs
8. Review and tighten service account permissions (least privilege)

LONG-TERM:
9. Deploy ZTNA for remote access (replace VPN)
10. Implement PAM solution for service account management
11. Enable Sophos MDR Complete for 24/7 threat hunting

items.title: IOCs (Indicators of Compromise)
items.content:
FILES:
• Q1_Budget_Review.xlsm — SHA256: 4a8b3f2e1d9c7b5a6e8f0d2c4b6a8e0f2d4c6b8a0e2f4d6c8b0a2e4f6d8c0b
• update_helper.dll — SHA256: 7c9d1e3f5a2b4d6e8f0a2c4d6e8b0f2a4c6d8e0b2a4c6d8e0f2a4c6d8e0b2a
• svchost_alt.exe — SHA256: 2b4d6e8f0a2c4d6e8b0f2a4c6d8e0b2a4c6d8e0f2a4c6d8e0b2a4c6d8e0f2a

NETWORK:
• C2 Server: 185.220.101.47:443 (HTTPS)
• Cobalt Strike Malleable C2 Profile: jquery-c2.4.7
• User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 10.0; Windows NT 6.2)

PERSISTENCE:
• Scheduled Task: WindowsHealthCheck (schtasks /create /tn)
• Registry: HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\SecurityUpdate
• Drop path: C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Crypto\RSA\MachineKeys\

CREDENTIALS:
• Compromised: svc-backup (domain service account)
• Method: Mimikatz sekurlsa::logonpasswords

extraActivities.action: Case created — ransomware indicators detected on DESKTOP-FIN042. MDR analyst assigned.
C2 beacon identified: rundll32.exe connecting to 185.220.101.47:443 (Cobalt Strike infrastructure). Monitoring lateral movement.
Lateral movement attempt blocked: DESKTOP-FIN042 → SRV-FS01 via SMB using svc-backup credentials. File server protected.
DESKTOP-FIN042 isolated from network. CryptoGuard blocked ransomware encryption — 127 files protected.
Firewall rule deployed: outbound traffic to 185.220.101.47 blocked across all network zones.
Compromised credentials identified and flagged for reset: svc-backup (service account used for lateral movement).
Remediation complete: Malicious files removed (Q1_Budget_Review.xlsm, msupdate.dll, beacon payload). Scheduled task persistence cleared. Registry modifications reverted.

Incident report generated and delivered to customer. DESKTOP-FIN042 cleared for reconnection pending customer approval. Recommended actions: reset svc-backup password, review email security policies, enable MFA on service accounts.

queryResults.query: SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, p.parent, pp.name AS parent_name FROM processes p LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid WHERE p.cmdline LIKE '%rundll32%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%beacon%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%185.220.101%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%vssadmin%' OR p.cmdline LIKE '%shadow%' OR p.name = 'schtasks.exe'
queryResults.queryName: MDR Threat Hunt — Cobalt Strike & Ransomware IOCs
